annotations.label: Coxeter group (presentation)

anchor.snippet: Formally, a Coxeter group can be defined as a group with the presentation

annotations.note: CoxeterMatrix.Group is defined as PresentedGroup M.relationsSet with relations (s_i s_{i'})^{M i i'}, exactly the Coxeter presentation.

annotations.label: Coxeter system

anchor.snippet: is called a Coxeter system

annotations.note: CoxeterSystem M W records a group isomorphism between W and the Coxeter group of M, the standard notion of a Coxeter system.

annotations.label: Generators are involutions

anchor.snippet: as such the generators are involutions

annotations.note: simple_mul_simple_self proves s i * s i = 1 (and simple_sq gives (s i)^2 = 1), exactly that each generator is an involution.

annotations.label: Commuting generators

anchor.snippet: then the generators

annotations.note: simple_mul_simple_pow gives (s i * s i')^{M i i'} = 1 (so commuting when M=2), but there is no explicit Commute lemma stated for that case.

annotations.label: Coxeter matrix

anchor.snippet: symmetric matrix with entries

annotations.note: CoxeterMatrix is a symmetric ℕ-matrix with diagonal 1 and off-diagonal ≠ 1, matching the article (using 0 in place of ∞).

annotations.label: Coxeter diagram

anchor.snippet: can be conveniently encoded by a Coxeter diagram

annotations.note: Mathlib draws specific Coxeter–Dynkin diagrams only in docstrings; there is no general Coxeter diagram graph object defined.

annotations.label: Commute iff no edge

anchor.snippet: two generators commute if and only if they are not joined by an edge

annotations.note: No Coxeter diagram is defined and there is no iff lemma relating commuting of generators to absence of an edge.

annotations.label: Schläfli matrix

anchor.snippet: but the elements are modified, being proportional to the dot product of the pairwise generators

annotations.note: The cosine/Schläfli matrix -2cos(π/m_{ij}) and the geometric representation are only mentioned as a TODO in Coxeter/Basic, not defined.

annotations.label: Eigenvalues determine type

anchor.snippet: its eigenvalues determine whether the Coxeter group is of finite type

annotations.note: No Schläfli matrix or finite-type criterion via eigenvalues exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric group as Coxeter group

anchor.snippet: are placed in a row with each vertex joined by an unlabelled edge to its immediate neighbors

annotations.note: The type-A Coxeter matrix is defined, but no isomorphism between Equiv.Perm and its Coxeter group is proven (noted as a TODO).

annotations.label: Reflection groups are Coxeter groups

anchor.snippet: every reflection group may be presented as a Coxeter group

annotations.note: No construction presenting a reflection group as a Coxeter group exists (the geometric representation is only a TODO).

annotations.label: Finite Coxeter groups are reflection groups

anchor.snippet: every finite Coxeter group admits a faithful representation as a finite reflection group of some Euclidean space

annotations.note: No faithful Euclidean reflection representation of finite Coxeter groups is formalized.

annotations.label: Classification by Coxeter diagrams

anchor.snippet: Finite Coxeter groups are classified in terms of their Coxeter diagrams

annotations.note: The finite-type Coxeter matrices (A,B,D,I,E₆,E₇,E₈,F₄,G₂,H₃,H₄) are defined, but the classification theorem is explicitly stated as unproven in the docstring.

annotations.label: Finite irreducible Coxeter group families

anchor.snippet: The finite Coxeter groups with connected Coxeter diagrams consist of three one-parameter families of increasing dimension

annotations.note: The families CoxeterMatrix.A/B/D and exceptional matrices are defined, but the enumeration theorem of finite irreducible types is not proven.

annotations.label: Weyl groups are Coxeter groups

anchor.snippet: every Weyl group can be realized as a Coxeter group

annotations.note: RootPairing.weylGroup is defined, but no result realizing it as a Coxeter group is formalized.

annotations.label: Coxeter groups are automatic

anchor.snippet: every finitely generated Coxeter group is an automatic group

annotations.note: Automatic groups are not defined anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: B_n and C_n share Weyl group

anchor.snippet: give rise to the same Weyl group

annotations.note: No statement comparing the Weyl groups of types B_n and C_n is formalized.

annotations.label: Regular polytope symmetry is finite Coxeter group

anchor.snippet: The symmetry group of every regular polytope is a finite Coxeter group

annotations.note: No theory of regular polytopes or their symmetry groups as Coxeter groups exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simplex symmetry group (type A_n)

anchor.snippet: The symmetry group of a regular n -simplex is the symmetric group S n +1

annotations.note: CoxeterMatrix.A is documented as the simplex symmetry group of type A_n, but no proof identifying it with the symmetric group S_{n+1} exists.

annotations.label: Hyperoctahedral group (type B_n)

anchor.snippet: is known as the hyperoctahedral group

annotations.note: CoxeterMatrix.B is defined (hypercube/orthoplex symmetry), but the hyperoctahedral group is not separately defined or identified with it.

annotations.label: Dihedral groups I_2(p)

anchor.snippet: the dihedral groups , which are the symmetry groups of regular polygons , form the series I 2 ( p ), for p ≥ 3

annotations.note: CoxeterMatrix.I (type I₂(m)) and DihedralGroup both exist, but no proven isomorphism connects the dihedral group to its Coxeter group.

annotations.label: Semiregular polytope symmetry groups

anchor.snippet: are the symmetry groups of certain semiregular polytopes

annotations.note: Semiregular polytopes and their symmetry groups (types D, E) are not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine Coxeter group structure

anchor.snippet: each contains a normal abelian subgroup such that the corresponding quotient group is finite

annotations.note: Affine Coxeter groups and their normal abelian (translation) subgroup structure are not formalized.

annotations.label: Stiefel diagram and affine Coxeter group

anchor.snippet: one can construct the associated Stiefel diagram

annotations.note: No Stiefel diagram or affine Coxeter group construction exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Free transitive action on alcoves

anchor.snippet: the affine Coxeter group acts freely and transitively on the alcoves

annotations.note: Alcoves and the simply transitive action of affine Coxeter groups are not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine Coxeter generators via roots

anchor.snippet: the affine Coxeter group is generated by the ordinary (linear) reflections about the hyperplanes perpendicular to

annotations.note: There is no formalization of affine Coxeter groups generated by reflections in root hyperplanes.

annotations.label: Infinitely many hyperbolic Coxeter groups

anchor.snippet: There are infinitely many hyperbolic Coxeter groups describing reflection groups in hyperbolic space

annotations.note: Hyperbolic Coxeter groups and reflection groups in hyperbolic space are not formalized.

annotations.label: Irreducible Coxeter group

anchor.snippet: A Coxeter group is said to be irreducible if its Coxeter–Dynkin diagram is connected

annotations.note: Irreducibility of Coxeter groups (via diagram connectedness) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Direct product decomposition

anchor.snippet: Every Coxeter group is the direct product of the irreducible groups that correspond to the components of its Coxeter–Dynkin diagram

annotations.note: The direct-product decomposition of a Coxeter group into irreducible components is not formalized.

annotations.label: Length function

anchor.snippet: gives rise to a length function ℓ on a Coxeter group

annotations.note: CoxeterSystem.length is the minimal number of simple reflections whose product is w, the standard length function.

annotations.label: Reduced word

anchor.snippet: An expression for v using ℓ ( v ) generators is a reduced word

annotations.note: CoxeterSystem.IsReduced ω holds iff ℓ(π ω) = ω.length, exactly the notion of a reduced word.

annotations.label: Reduced words of (13) in S_3

anchor.snippet: the permutation (13) in S 3 has two reduced words

annotations.note: S_3 is not set up as a Coxeter system, so this specific enumeration of reduced words is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sign map generalization

anchor.snippet: generalizing the sign map for the symmetric group

annotations.note: CoxeterSystem.lengthParity : W →* Multiplicative (ZMod 2) is the homomorphism w ↦ (-1)^{ℓ(w)}, generalizing the symmetric-group sign map.

annotations.label: Three partial orders

anchor.snippet: Using reduced words one may define three partial orders on the Coxeter group

annotations.note: Neither Bruhat order nor the left/right weak orders on a Coxeter group are defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bruhat order

anchor.snippet: An element v exceeds an element u in the Bruhat order if some

annotations.note: The Bruhat order on a Coxeter group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Weak order

anchor.snippet: In the weak order, v ≥ u if some reduced word for v contains a reduced word for u as an initial segment

annotations.note: The weak (left/right) order on a Coxeter group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Reduced word of (1 2 3) in S_3

anchor.snippet: the permutation (1 2 3) in S 3 has only one reduced word

annotations.note: S_3 is not realized as a Coxeter system, so this specific reduced-word example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Abelianization is elementary abelian 2-group

anchor.snippet: its abelianization is an elementary abelian 2-group

annotations.note: There is no result that the abelianization of a Coxeter group is an elementary abelian 2-group.

annotations.label: Schur multiplier is elementary abelian 2-group

anchor.snippet: the Schur multiplier is also an elementary abelian 2-group

annotations.note: The Schur multiplier of a Coxeter group is not computed or even referenced in Mathlib.
